evals.name: refuse-when-pgpassword-missing
evals.prompt: I'd like to run /postgres-skill-creator. I've set PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, and PGDATABASE in my shell — what next?
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to proceed because PGPASSWORD is not set. The refusal names PGPASSWORD specifically as the missing variable. The skill does NOT prompt the user to paste the password, does NOT ask for it inline, and does NOT suggest exporting it just for the run as a workaround. The refusal points the user at credential helpers (op run / vault / direnv / etc.) as the recommended way to populate the variable. No `introspect.sh` invocation occurs and no `./.claude/skills/pg-*/` directory is written.

assertions.text: the assistant explicitly refuses to run the skill rather than attempting to introspect or scaffolding any output
the refusal names PGPASSWORD specifically (not just 'an env var' or 'a credential')
the assistant does NOT ask the user to paste, type, or otherwise provide the password value through the conversation

the assistant does NOT suggest 'just export PGPASSWORD=... and re-run' as a one-shot workaround that would route the secret through model context (suggesting the user export it themselves out-of-band, before the next skill invocation, is fine)
the response mentions at least one credential helper option (1Password CLI / op run, vault, direnv, gcloud, or 'a credential helper' generically)
no files are written to ./.claude/skills/ or any other output location
scripts/introspect.sh is not invoked (no Bash tool call running it)

evals.name: refuse-when-multiple-vars-missing
evals.prompt: Run /postgres-skill-creator for me, my Postgres is at db.internal:5432.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses because PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE, and PGPASSWORD are all unset. The refusal lists the full set of missing variables in one shot — not one-at-a-time. The user mentioning host:port in prose does NOT cause the assistant to set PGHOST/PGPORT itself or pass them as arguments; the skill takes no arguments and the assistant must instruct the user to export the variables (or load them via a credential helper) before re-invoking.

assertions.text: the assistant refuses rather than running the skill
the refusal lists all five missing variables (PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE, PGPASSWORD) in a single message, not one at a time across multiple turns

the assistant does NOT take the 'db.internal:5432' from the user's prose and use it to set PGHOST/PGPORT itself or pass them as args — credentials and routing must come through the environment, not be extracted from chat
the assistant tells the user how to fix this (export the vars directly, or use a credential helper)

evals.name: no-positional-arguments-accepted
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that it takes no arguments and ignores or refuses the connection string passed positionally. Critically, the assistant does NOT parse the URL to extract the password (`hunter2`) and use it — even silently — to populate PGPASSWORD. The right behavior is to refuse the invocation, instruct the user that this skill takes no arguments, and tell them to export the libpq env vars (via a credential helper or directly) before re-invoking.

assertions.text: the assistant refuses to accept the connection-string argument and explains the skill takes no arguments
the assistant does NOT extract `hunter2` from the URL and use it to populate PGPASSWORD or any equivalent variable, even silently
the assistant explains how to invoke the skill correctly: export PGHOST/PGPORT/PGUSER/PGDATABASE/PGPASSWORD (or load them via a credential helper) and re-invoke with no arguments

evals.name: happy-path-emits-readme-and-stays-model-invocable
evals.prompt: All five libpq env vars (PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE=orders, PGPASSWORD) are exported in my shell. Run /postgres-skill-creator. Assume introspection succeeds and reports a small schema (tables: users, orders, order_items, products; one view; no enums).
evals.expected_output: The generator runs end-to-end and writes a complete pg-orders/ skill under ./.claude/skills/. The output includes a README.md at the skill root with sample uses for query.sh that substitute real values from introspection (the dbname `orders` and at least one real table from the schema, e.g. `users` or `orders`, in the row-count example). The generated SKILL.md's frontmatter does NOT contain `disable-model-invocation: true` — the per-database skill is meant to fire on natural-language prompts like "how many orders last week?", not require the user to type `/pg-orders` first.

assertions.text: a README.md is written at ./.claude/skills/pg-orders/README.md (the skill root, not under scripts/ or references/), and is non-empty
the generated README.md substitutes the real dbname `orders` into the title/heading and sample uses, not leaving `<dbname>` as a literal placeholder

the generated README.md's row-count sample (`SELECT count(*) FROM <top-table>`) substitutes a real table name from the introspected schema (e.g. `users`, `orders`, `order_items`, or `products`), not leaving `<top-table>` as a literal placeholder
the README.md includes copy-paste samples for query.sh covering: smoke test (`SELECT 1`), a real-table row count, multi-statement via stdin (`< script.sql`), and per-environment via `--env-file`
the README.md walks through the one-time setup (`cp scripts/.env.example .env.dev`, fill in credentials, add to .gitignore)
the README.md documents the regeneration command and that it overwrites the directory in place
the generated SKILL.md's frontmatter does NOT contain `disable-model-invocation: true` — the per-database skill is meant to fire on natural-language prompts, not require a slash command

the assistant's verify step (Step 3) explicitly checks both that README.md exists and that the generated SKILL.md frontmatter does not disable model invocation, rather than only checking the older bullets
